decisionMakers.name: Kerry Devine
decisionMakers.note: Revenue, school funding, wastewater, recycled water, and tax pressure.

decisionMakers.name: Will Mackintosh
decisionMakers.note: Labor can influence him; water remains a major concern.

decisionMakers.name: Jannan Holmes
decisionMakers.note: Former school board member; may follow Mackintosh's position.

decisionMakers.name: Matt Rowe
decisionMakers.note: Democratic and environmental networks matter; former school board member.

decisionMakers.name: Joy Crump
decisionMakers.note: Business issues and avoiding tax increases are central messages.

decisionMakers.name: Susanna Finn
decisionMakers.note: Workforce housing is a priority; labor outreach matters.

decisionMakers.name: Charlie Frye
decisionMakers.note: Influenced by employment opportunities and education funding.

risks.title: Resident quality-of-life opposition
risks.description: 48 general-opposition stakeholders are tracked, with concerns around noise, forest removal, water, traffic, and quality of life.
risks.mitigation: Continue direct outreach, document mitigation commitments, and recruit nearby validators.

risks.title: Environmental and water advocacy
risks.description: 6 conservation stakeholders and 9 opposition groups are tracked, including FOR, Sierra Club, and Inform Fredericksburg.
risks.mitigation: Prepare water, environmental, and stormwater facts before public meetings.

risks.title: Social media narrative
risks.description: 2 social-media opposition stakeholders are tracked, including Inform Fredericksburg contacts.
risks.mitigation: Monitor public claims and keep council-facing answers concise and sourced.

risks.title: Site-adjacent access concerns
risks.description: 1 business stakeholder is categorized as opposition with access and operational concerns.
risks.mitigation: Engage directly with affected business and address access concerns in project documentation.
